Introduction to DECODE

# Load DECODE after installation:
# devtools::install_github("dinhngockhanh/DECODE")
# library(DECODE)

# Or source the R scripts from a local clone of the repository:
r_dir <- file.path("..", "R")
if (dir.exists(r_dir)) {
    r_files <- list.files(r_dir, pattern = "[.]r$|[.]R$", full.names = TRUE)
    invisible(lapply(r_files, source))
}

set.seed(1)
options(max.print = 100)

sample_id <- "example_sample"
data_dir <- "sim_data"
output_dir <- "DECODE_results"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

Introduction

DECODE (Deciphering Cancer Origin from DNA Evolution) deconvolves bulk tumor mutations into a neutral tail and one or more mutation clusters based on their Variant Allele Frequencies (VAFs). The algorithm corrects for sample-specific sequencing coverage and mutation-calling biases when building the Site Frequency Spectrum (SFS).

This vignette walks through a complete hands-on workflow:

  1. Prepare a mutation table and a copy-number segment file.
  2. Annotate each mutation with its local copy number using findInterval.
  3. Retain diploid loci (nMajor == 1 and nMinor == 1) and run DECODE.
  4. Save the result and generate diagnostic plots.

Throughout this tutorial we use the example files in sim_data/:

  • mutation_table.csv
  • copy_number_segments.txt

1. Prepare input data

DECODE requires a mutation table with at least Ref_count and Alt_count. In practice, you also need genomic coordinates so that each mutation can be matched to a copy-number segment.

1a. Mutation table

The mutation table must contain integer read counts for the reference and alternate alleles. Additional columns such as chromosome and position are needed for copy-number annotation, but are not passed directly to DECODE().

Below is the expected format (first few rows):

mutation_table <- read.csv(file.path(data_dir, "mutation_table.csv"))
head(mutation_table)
#>   Chromosome Position Ref_count Alt_count
#> 1       chr1  1776022        73        46
#> 2       chr1  2807530        54        40
#> 3       chr1  3393561        88        25
#> 4       chr1  4079721        61        30
#> 5       chr1  4482488        71        49
#> 6       chr1  4821082        67        46

Required columns:

Column Description
Ref_count Number of reads supporting the reference allele
Alt_count Number of reads supporting the alternate allele

Typical optional columns:

Column Description
Chromosome Chromosome name (e.g. chr1, chr2, …)
Position Genomic position on that chromosome

If your data come from a VCF or other format, convert them to this structure before continuing. For example, if you have total depth and alternate count, set Ref_count = total_depth - Alt_count.

1b. Copy-number segment file

Copy-number segments define the major and minor allele copy number along each chromosome. DECODE should be run on mutations in diploid regions (nMajor == 1, nMinor == 1), where VAF directly reflects clonal frequency.

The example file uses tab-separated values with the following columns:

cn_segments <- read.delim(file.path(data_dir, "copy_number_segments.txt"))
head(cn_segments)
#>   chr  startpos    endpos nMajor nMinor
#> 1   1     61735 156702010      1      1
#> 2   1 156709407 157110875      1      0
#> 3   1 157113750 157618602      0      0
#> 4   1 157621560 159544391      1      0
#> 5   1 159545505 248930189      1      1
#> 6   2     12784 155456532      1      1

Required columns:

Column Description
chr Chromosome identifier (e.g. 1, 2, …, X)
startpos Start coordinate of the segment (bp)
endpos End coordinate of the segment (bp)
nMajor Copy number of the major allele
nMinor Copy number of the minor allele

Segments on the same chromosome must not overlap, and should be sorted by startpos.

Note on chromosome naming. Mutation tables often use a chr prefix (chr1), while copy-number files may use bare chromosome names (1). We normalize chromosome labels in the next section before matching.

2. Annotate mutations with copy number

We assign each mutation to the copy-number segment that contains its genomic position. For each chromosome, findInterval() locates the segment whose startpos is less than or equal to the mutation position.

annotate_mutations_with_cn <- function(mutation_table, cn_segments) {
    if (!all(c("Chromosome", "Position") %in% colnames(mutation_table))) {
        stop("mutation_table must contain columns 'Chromosome' and 'Position'.")
    }
    if (!all(c("chr", "startpos", "endpos", "nMajor", "nMinor") %in% colnames(cn_segments))) {
        stop("cn_segments must contain columns 'chr', 'startpos', 'endpos', 'nMajor', and 'nMinor'.")
    }

    mutation_table <- mutation_table
    mutation_table$chr <- sub("^chr", "", as.character(mutation_table$Chromosome))
    cn_segments <- cn_segments
    cn_segments$chr <- as.character(cn_segments$chr)

    mutation_table$nMajor <- NA_integer_
    mutation_table$nMinor <- NA_integer_

    for (chr in unique(mutation_table$chr)) {
        mut_idx <- which(mutation_table$chr == chr)
        cn_chr <- cn_segments[cn_segments$chr == chr, , drop = FALSE]
        if (nrow(cn_chr) == 0) next

        cn_chr <- cn_chr[order(cn_chr$startpos), , drop = FALSE]
        pos <- mutation_table$Position[mut_idx]
        seg_idx <- findInterval(pos, cn_chr$startpos)

        # Keep only positions that fall inside the matched segment
        valid <- seg_idx > 0
        valid[valid] <- pos[valid] <= cn_chr$endpos[seg_idx[valid]]
        mutation_table$nMajor[mut_idx[valid]] <- cn_chr$nMajor[seg_idx[valid]]
        mutation_table$nMinor[mut_idx[valid]] <- cn_chr$nMinor[seg_idx[valid]]
    }

    return(mutation_table)
}

Apply the annotation:

mutation_table_cn <- annotate_mutations_with_cn(
    mutation_table = mutation_table,
    cn_segments = cn_segments
)

cat(
    "Total mutations:", nrow(mutation_table_cn), "\n",
    "Mapped to a CN segment:", sum(!is.na(mutation_table_cn$nMajor)), "\n"
)
#> Total mutations: 4623 
#>  Mapped to a CN segment: 4421
head(mutation_table_cn[, c("Chromosome", "Position", "Ref_count", "Alt_count", "nMajor", "nMinor")])
#>   Chromosome Position Ref_count Alt_count nMajor nMinor
#> 1       chr1  1776022        73        46      1      1
#> 2       chr1  2807530        54        40      1      1
#> 3       chr1  3393561        88        25      1      1
#> 4       chr1  4079721        61        30      1      1
#> 5       chr1  4482488        71        49      1      1
#> 6       chr1  4821082        67        46      1      1

Mutations with missing copy-number values lie outside the supplied segment file (for example, on chromosomes not present in the CN table) and should be removed before filtering.

mutation_table_cn <- mutation_table_cn[!is.na(mutation_table_cn$nMajor), ]
nrow(mutation_table_cn)
#> [1] 4421

3. Select diploid mutations for DECODE

DECODE models clonal structure from VAF under the assumption that each mutation occurs on a single haploid copy in a diploid locus. Therefore, retain only mutations with nMajor == 1 and nMinor == 1:

mutation_table_diploid <- mutation_table_cn[
    mutation_table_cn$nMajor == 1 & mutation_table_cn$nMinor == 1,
]

cat(
    "Diploid mutations (nMajor = 1, nMinor = 1):", nrow(mutation_table_diploid), "\n"
)
#> Diploid mutations (nMajor = 1, nMinor = 1): 4004

Remove mutations with zero reference or alternate read counts, and keep only the columns required by DECODE():

mutation_table_decode <- mutation_table_diploid[
    mutation_table_diploid$Ref_count > 0 & mutation_table_diploid$Alt_count > 0,
    c("Ref_count", "Alt_count")
]

head(mutation_table_decode)
#>   Ref_count Alt_count
#> 1        73        46
#> 2        54        40
#> 3        88        25
#> 4        61        30
#> 5        71        49
#> 6        67        46
nrow(mutation_table_decode)
#> [1] 4004

DECODE needs a sufficient number of mutations to estimate the SFS reliably. If fewer than roughly 100 mutations remain after filtering, consider relaxing upstream filters or checking copy-number inference quality.

4. Run DECODE

The main function is DECODE(). It accepts the filtered mutation table and returns a list object with model-selection results, best-fit parameters, and SFS summaries.

Key arguments include:

  • sample_id: label for the sample.
  • mutation_table: data frame with Ref_count and Alt_count.
  • neutral_tail: whether to include a neutral tail (NA = compare with and without tail automatically).
  • N_clusters_min, N_clusters_max: range of cluster counts to evaluate.
  • ABCSMCDRF_nParticles: number of ABC-SMC-DRF particles per iteration (larger values improve accuracy but increase runtime).
DECODE_result <- DECODE(
    sample_id = sample_id,
    mutation_table = mutation_table_decode,
    neutral_tail = NA,
    N_clusters_min = 1,
    N_clusters_max = 3,
    ABCSMCDRF_nParticles = rep(500, 5)
)
#> 
#> 
#> 
#> PERFORMING DECODE FOR SAMPLE example_sample...
#> Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
#> Warning: package 'data.table' was built under R version 4.5.2
#> Choose mutation thresholds for inference and validation...

#> Validation score       : GIC = 0.17 ± 0 (L1 error = 0.14 ± 0, penalty = 0.03 ± 0)
#> Neutral component      : α   = 1.11 ± 0.01; mutation count: 6216 ± 81 [True], 1693 ± 18 [A], 1345 ± 18 [B] 
#> Cluster 1              : f   = 0.40 ± 0.00; mutation count: 2144 ± 18 [True], 2074 ± 18 [A], 2132 ± 18 [B] 
#> Inference for 2 clusters with neutral tail component...
#>   |                                                          |                                                  |   0%  |                                                          |++++++++++                                        |  20%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++                              |  40%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++                    |  60%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++          |  80%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 100%
#>   |                                                          |                                                  |   0%  |                                                          |++++++++++                                        |  20%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++                              |  40%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++                    |  60%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++          |  80%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 100%
#> Validation score       : GIC = 0.16 ± 0 (L1 error = 0.11 ± 0, penalty = 0.06 ± 0)

#> Cluster 1              : f   = 0.41 ± 0.00; mutation count: 1960 ± 4 [True], 1895 ± 4 [A], 1950 ± 4 [B] 
#> Cluster 2              : f   = 0.31 ± 0.00; mutation count: 414 ± 9 [True], 400 ± 9 [A], 409 ± 9 [B] 
#> H = 2 vs H = 1         : Wilcoxon p-value = 0 → continue with higher cluster counts
#> Inference for 3 clusters with neutral tail component...
#>   |                                                          |                                                  |   0%  |                                                          |++++++++++                                        |  20%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++                              |  40%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++                    |  60%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++          |  80%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 100%
#>   |                                                          |                                                  |   0%  |                                                          |++++++++++                                        |  20%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++                              |  40%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++                    |  60%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++          |  80%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 100%
#> Validation score       : GIC = 0.16 ± 0 (L1 error = 0.08 ± 0, penalty = 0.08 ± 0)
#> Neutral component      : α   = 1.31 ± 0.02; mutation count: 6582 ± 164 [True], 1098 ± 28 [A], 826 ± 26 [B] 
#> Cluster 1              : f   = 0.40 ± 0.00; mutation count: 2069 ± 9 [True], 2001 ± 9 [A], 2059 ± 9 [B] 
#> Cluster 2              : f   = 0.29 ± 0.00; mutation count: 405 ± 14 [True], 392 ± 13 [A], 400 ± 14 [B] 
#> Cluster 3              : f   = 0.06 ± 0.00; mutation count: 378 ± 18 [True], 289 ± 15 [A], 195 ± 12 [B] 
#> H = 3 vs H = 2         : Wilcoxon p-value = 8.39e-302 → maximum cluster count has been reached, increased N_clusters_max may improve fit
#> Inference for 1 cluster without neutral tail component...
#>   |                                                          |                                                  |   0%  |                                                          |++++++++++                                        |  20%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++                              |  40%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++                    |  60%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++          |  80%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 100%
#> Validation score       : GIC = 0.46 ± 0 (L1 error = 0.44 ± 0, penalty = 0.02 ± 0)
#> Cluster 1              : f   = 0.40 ± 0.00; mutation count: 2312 ± 0 [True], 2236 ± 0 [A], 2299 ± 0 [B] 
#> Inference for 2 clusters without neutral tail component...
#>   |                                                          |                                                  |   0%  |                                                          |++++++++++                                        |  20%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++                              |  40%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++                    |  60%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++          |  80%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 100%
#>   |                                                          |                                                  |   0%  |                                                          |++++++++++                                        |  20%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++                              |  40%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++                    |  60%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++          |  80%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 100%
#> Validation score       : GIC = 0.32 ± 0 (L1 error = 0.27 ± 0, penalty = 0.05 ± 0)
#> Cluster 1              : f   = 0.39 ± 0.00; mutation count: 2272 ± 9 [True], 2198 ± 8 [A], 2260 ± 9 [B] 
#> Cluster 2              : f   = 0.07 ± 0.00; mutation count: 1046 ± 26 [True], 830 ± 18 [A], 591 ± 11 [B] 
#> H = 2 vs H = 1         : Wilcoxon p-value = 0 → continue with higher cluster counts
#> Inference for 3 clusters without neutral tail component...
#>   |                                                          |                                                  |   0%  |                                                          |++++++++++                                        |  20%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++                              |  40%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++                    |  60%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++          |  80%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 100%
#>   |                                                          |                                                  |   0%  |                                                          |++++++++++                                        |  20%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++                              |  40%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++                    |  60%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++          |  80%  |                                                          |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 100%
#> Validation score       : GIC = 0.23 ± 0 (L1 error = 0.15 ± 0, penalty = 0.07 ± 0)
#> Cluster 1              : f   = 0.40 ± 0.00; mutation count: 2197 ± 3 [True], 2125 ± 3 [A], 2185 ± 3 [B] 
#> Cluster 2              : f   = 0.27 ± 0.00; mutation count: 587 ± 1 [True], 568 ± 1 [A], 577 ± 1 [B] 
#> Cluster 3              : f   = 0.06 ± 0.00; mutation count: 1074 ± 6 [True], 839 ± 7 [A], 584 ± 7 [B] 
#> H = 3 vs H = 2         : Wilcoxon p-value = 0 → maximum cluster count has been reached, increased N_clusters_max may improve fit
#> With vs without tail   : Wilcoxon p-value = 1 → fit with tail is selected
#> Best fit = neutral tail + 3 clusters:
#> Validation score       : GIC = 0.16 ± 0 (L1 error = 0.08 ± 0, penalty = 0.08 ± 0)
#> Neutral component      : α   = 1.31 ± 0.02; mutation count: 6582 ± 164 [True], 1098 ± 28 [A], 826 ± 26 [B] 
#> Cluster 1              : f   = 0.40 ± 0.00; mutation count: 2069 ± 9 [True], 2001 ± 9 [A], 2059 ± 9 [B] 
#> Cluster 2              : f   = 0.29 ± 0.00; mutation count: 405 ± 14 [True], 392 ± 13 [A], 400 ± 14 [B] 
#> Cluster 3              : f   = 0.06 ± 0.00; mutation count: 378 ± 18 [True], 289 ± 15 [A], 195 ± 12 [B]

The console output reports the selected model (with or without neutral tail), the optimal number of clusters, and parameter estimates for each component.

Inspect the main result fields:

names(DECODE_result)
#>  [1] "sample_id"                    "mutation_table"              
#>  [3] "criterion"                    "criterion_penalty_scale"     
#>  [5] "wilcoxon_pvalue_threshold"    "criterion_Nsamples"          
#>  [7] "criterion_tail_weight"        "neutral_power_min"           
#>  [9] "neutral_power_max"            "cluster_VAF_min"             
#> [11] "cluster_VAF_max"              "max_total_read"              
#> [13] "allele_count"                 "SFS_bincount"                
#> [15] "SFS_frequencies"              "ABCSMCDRF_nParticles"        
#> [17] "min_variant_read_inference_A" "min_total_read_inference_A"  
#> [19] "min_variant_read_inference_B" "min_total_read_inference_B"  
#> [21] "min_variant_read_validation"  "min_total_read_validation"   
#> [23] "inference_retained_freq"      "neutral_tail"                
#> [25] "N_clusters_min"               "N_clusters_max"              
#> [27] "compute_parallel"             "n_cores"                     
#> [29] "readcount_distribution"       "SFS_data_inference_A"        
#> [31] "SFS_data_inference_B"         "SFS_data_validation"         
#> [33] "fits_with_tail"               "fits_without_tail"           
#> [35] "best_with_tail"               "best_N_clusters"
DECODE_result$best_with_tail
#> [1] TRUE
DECODE_result$best_N_clusters
#> [1] 3

To retrieve parameters from the best-fitting model:

if (DECODE_result$best_with_tail) {
    fit_key <- paste0("fits_with_tail")
} else {
    fit_key <- paste0("fits_without_tail")
}
best_fit <- DECODE_result[[fit_key]]$all_fits[[
    paste0(DECODE_result$best_N_clusters, "_clusters")
]]
names(best_fit)
#>  [1] "N_clusters"                "with_tail"                
#>  [3] "SFS_inference_A"           "SFS_inference_B"          
#>  [5] "SFS_validation"            "parameters"               
#>  [7] "posterior_parameters"      "distances"                
#>  [9] "SFS_inference_A_tail"      "SFS_inference_B_tail"     
#> [11] "SFS_validation_tail"       "SFS_inference_A_cluster_1"
#> [13] "SFS_inference_B_cluster_1" "SFS_validation_cluster_1" 
#> [15] "SFS_inference_A_cluster_2" "SFS_inference_B_cluster_2"
#> [17] "SFS_validation_cluster_2"  "SFS_inference_A_cluster_3"
#> [19] "SFS_inference_B_cluster_3" "SFS_validation_cluster_3" 
#> [21] "criterion_values"

For production analyses, use the default ABCSMCDRF_nParticles = rep(1000, 10) and a wider N_clusters_max if needed. The reduced settings above keep vignette rendering time reasonable.

5. Save the DECODE result

Save the full result object as an .rds file so you can reload it later without rerunning inference:

result_file <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("DECODE_", sample_id, ".rds"))
saveRDS(DECODE_result, file = result_file)
result_file
#> [1] "DECODE_results/DECODE_example_sample.rds"

Reload a saved result in a new R session:

DECODE_result <- readRDS(result_file)

6. Visualize DECODE output

DECODE provides several plotting functions in DECODE_plots.r:

  • DECODE_plot_SFS(): observed vs. fitted SFS for the best model.
  • DECODE_plot_model_selection(): comparison of candidate models (with/without tail, different cluster counts).
  • DECODE_plot_criteria(): model-selection criteria across candidates.
  • DECODE_plot_readcounts(): mutation read-count distribution with DECODE filtering thresholds.

Both DECODE_plot_SFS() and DECODE_plot_model_selection() accept optional filename, width, height, units, and res arguments. Their defaults are width = 30, height = 15, units = "in", and res = 150 (i.e. 4500 × 2250 pixels when saved as PNG). The plot layout and font sizes in DECODE_plots.r are calibrated for this canvas size.

When embedding plots in an HTML vignette, save the PNG first and then reference the saved file in the document. Re-drawing the ggplot or grid object inside a small default knitr device (~7 × 7 inches) compresses the same absolute font sizes into a much smaller figure and causes clipping. The workflow below writes PNG files to DECODE_results/ and embeds those files directly, so the HTML output matches the on-disk figures.

6a. Site Frequency Spectrum plot

sfs_file <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("DECODE_SFS_", sample_id))

DECODE_plot_SFS(
    DECODE_result = DECODE_result,
    filename = sfs_file,
    filetype = "png"
)

The PNG is saved to DECODE_results/DECODE_SFS_example_sample.png at 30 × 15 inches and 150 dpi.

6b. Model selection plot

model_selection_file <- file.path(
    output_dir,
    paste0("DECODE_model_selection_", sample_id)
)

DECODE_plot_model_selection(
    DECODE_result = DECODE_result,
    filename = model_selection_file,
    filetype = "png"
)

6c. Optional diagnostic plots

Criteria and read-count plots can help assess model choice and data filtering:

print(DECODE_plot_criteria(DECODE_result = DECODE_result))
print(DECODE_plot_readcounts(DECODE_result = DECODE_result))

Summary

This workflow covered the standard DECODE analysis path:

  1. Load a mutation table (Ref_count, Alt_count, plus genomic coordinates).
  2. Load copy-number segments (chr, startpos, endpos, nMajor, nMinor).
  3. Annotate each mutation with copy number via findInterval.
  4. Filter to diploid loci (nMajor == 1, nMinor == 1) and run DECODE().
  5. Save the result with saveRDS().
  6. Generate SFS and model-selection plots with DECODE_plot_SFS() and DECODE_plot_model_selection().

For your own data, replace the paths in data_dir with your mutation table and copy-number segment files, then repeat the same steps.
